title: What to know about Equisetum arvense before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Equisetum arvense, commonly known as scouring rush, is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine. It is primarily used to treat kidney and urinary issues, including urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The main health benefits of this herb include its anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic properties. Its therapeutic properties include being a natural anti-coagulant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic. Equisetum arvense contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Herbal preparations like teas and infusions are commonly made from this herb.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Equisetum arvense are its application in relieving arthritis through its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Rheumatism symptoms are also alleviated by Equisetum arvense, as it contains equisetic acid, a compound that reduces inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones, possibly due to its diuretic properties, which are a result of the presence of glycosides and saponins. It is also effective in treating diarrhea, thanks to its anti-diarrheal properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds. Additionally, Equisetum arvense is used to treat gout, urinary tract infections, diabetes, digestive issues, inflammation, and prostate issues, with its effectiveness in these conditions potentially linked to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for arthritis because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the joints.
This plant is used for rheumatism because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic properties, which can help alleviate joint pain and swelling.

This plant is used for kidney stones because it has diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and help flush out the kidneys and urinary tract, potentially relieving pain and discomfort.
This plant is used for diarrhea because it has anti-diarrheal properties, which can help slow down bowel movements and reduce the frequency of loose stools, helping to alleviate symptoms.
This plant is used for gout because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and uricosuric properties, which can help reduce uric acid levels and alleviate joint pain and swelling.

This plant is used for urinary tract infections because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and kill off bacteria in the urinary tract, potentially relieving symptoms and preventing further infection.
This plant is used for diabetes because it has hypoglycemic properties, which can help lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, potentially alleviating symptoms of the condition.

This plant is used for digestive issues because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome.

This plant is used for inflammation because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain and discomfort associated with inflammation.

This plant is used for prostate issues because it has anti-inflammatory and anti-androgenic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of prostate enlargement and other prostate disorders.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Equisetum arvense is used to treat various conditions, including arthritis, a condition characterized by joint pain and inflammation, often alleviated by the plant's anti-inflammatory properties. Rheumatism, a broader term for muscle and joint pain, is also commonly treated with Equisetum arvense, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms. Additionally, the plant has been used to help manage gout, a form of arthritis that causes sudden, severe joint pain, by reducing uric acid levels in the body. Furthermore, Equisetum arvense has been used to prevent kidney stones by reducing the concentration of uric acid in the urine, and to alleviate urinary tract infections by its antimicrobial properties. It has also been used to treat eczema, inflammation, urinary incontinence, muscle spasms, and osteoarthritis, a degenerative joint disease.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Equisetum arvense is used to treat a variety of conditions, including diarrhea, which is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to regulate bowel movements. Equisetum arvense is also employed to combat rheumatism, as its flavonoids and caffeic acid have shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant is used to address digestive issues, such as bloating and gas, due to its ability to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. Furthermore, its diuretic properties make it effective in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones, by increasing urine production and helping to break down stone formations. Equisetum arvense is also utilized to manage inflammation, arthritis, respiratory issues, menstrual issues, and prostate issues, with its flavonoids and caffeic acid exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Equisetum arvense is used to treat arthritis by reducing inflammation and alleviating joint pain. It is also applied to urinary tract infections to prevent bacterial growth and promote healing, as well as to help manage diabetes by stabilizing blood sugar levels and enhancing insulin sensitivity. Additionally, Equisetum arvense is used to treat rheumatism by reducing joint inflammation and improving mobility. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate kidney stones by preventing their formation and promoting the breakdown of kidney stones, and to manage gout by reducing uric acid levels. Equisetum arvense is also used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea, eczema, and skin infections by promoting digestion, reducing inflammation, and preventing bacterial growth.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Equisetum arvense is used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, which is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties. Rheumatism is also treated with this plant due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Gout, characterized by severe joint pain, is another condition that Equisetum arvense is used to treat, as it helps to reduce uric acid levels in the body. Furthermore, it is used to treat arthritis, urinary tract infections, kidney stones, fever, and menstrual disorders, as well as other conditions such as diabetes and menstrual issues, which are often associated with hormonal imbalances and inflammation.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Equisetum arvense is used to treat a variety of health conditions, including rheumatism, characterized by joint inflammation and pain, and arthritis, a chronic condition causing joint pain and stiffness. The plant is also used to alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, such as severe pain and nausea, and to provide relief from gout, a form of arthritis causing intense joint pain and swelling. Additionally, Equisetum arvense is used to address prostate issues, including enlarged prostate and benign prostatic hyperplasia, and to treat urinary tract infections, often accompanied by symptoms like burning urination and cloudy urine. It is also utilized in the treatment of cystitis, a type of urinary tract infection, as well as bladder inflammation, which can cause discomfort and pain during urination. Furthermore, Equisetum arvense is used to alleviate menstrual issues, such as cramps and heavy bleeding, and to provide relief from tendinitis, a condition causing pain and inflammation in tendons.
benefits_description: Equisetum arvense reduces inflammation by containing caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which inhibit prostaglandin synthesis, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort caused by inflammation. Relieving arthritis is also a notable benefit, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Equisetum arvense improves digestion due to its prebiotic properties, promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and enhancing nutrient absorption. Treating kidney stones involves the plant's ability to increase urine production and reduce stone formation, achieved through the diuretic and litholytic effects of its alkaloids. Equisetum arvense also eases joint pain, alleviates menstrual cramps, reduces fever, treats skin irritation, soothes irritable bowels, and relaxes muscle spasms, primarily due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the body. The plant's ability to reduce inflammation is attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties can help to relieve the pain and stiffness associated with arthritis. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of arthritis.

The plant contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help to improve digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have a protective effect on the gut and to improve the balance of gut bacteria.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat kidney stones due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the kidneys, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of kidney stones.

The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles and to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.

The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties can help to ease joint pain and stiffness. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the joints, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of joint pain.

The plant has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have an antipyretic effect, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of infection.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat skin irritation and other symptoms associated with eczema and other skin conditions. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have an anti-inflammatory effect on the skin, which can help to alleviate symptoms of skin irritation.

The plant's soothing effect on the digestive system can help to soothe irritable bowels and alleviate symptoms of IBS. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have a protective effect on the gut and to improve the balance of gut bacteria.
The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles, which can help to relax muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms of muscle tension.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Equisetum arvense improves blood flow by possessing vasodilatory properties, which contribute to its ability to enhance circulation. Equisetum arvense reduces blood pressure through the inhibition of angiotensin-converting enzyme and the reduction of aldosterone levels. This reduction in blood pressure is also linked to its anti-inflammatory properties, which in turn reduce inflammation, thereby improving oxygenation and reducing swelling in the body. Additionally, Equisetum arvense's antioxidant properties promote relaxation and reduce the formation of fibrin, preventing thrombosis and promoting the regeneration of damaged tissue. By possessing such a unique combination of properties, Equisetum arvense plays a significant role in maintaining a healthy circulatory system, ultimately preventing cardiovascular issues.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Equisetum arvense relieves inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Soothing digestive issues, Equisetum arvense has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and pathogenic microorganisms. Reducing bloating, the plant's anti-spasmodic properties calm muscle contractions in the digestive tract, allowing for more efficient digestion. Curing diarrhea, Equisetum arvense's anti-diarrheal properties slow down intestinal motility and restore the balance of gut flora. Treating colitis, Equisetum arvense's immunomodulatory properties modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gut.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Equisetum arvense regulates hormonal balance by its diuretic and antispasmodic properties, which help to remove excess fluids and relax the endocrine system. Stabilizing insulin levels is facilitated by its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the pancreas. Improving thyroid function is attributed to its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland's activity, as well as its anti-thyroid autoantibody properties, which inhibit the production of antibodies that attack the thyroid gland. Suppressing cortisol production is a result of its adaptogenic and anti-stress properties, which help the body to adapt to stress and reduce the production of cortisol. Equisetum arvense enhances adrenal function, maintains pituitary health, regenerates pancreatic tissue, inhibits estrogen dominance, reduces stress hormones, and supports parathyroid health, primarily through its adaptogenic, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Equisetum arvense soothes skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and swelling. It reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby promoting a healthy environment for skin repair. Treats acne breakouts by its antimicrobial action, preventing the growth of bacteria that cause acne and promoting the healing of affected skin. Equisetum arvense heals wound sites through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which enhance collagen synthesis and tissue repair. The plant relaxes muscle tension, solves dryness issues, cures eczema symptoms, fights fungal infections, prevents cell damage, and calms itching sensations, making it a versatile remedy for various integumentary system issues.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Equisetum arvense reduces swelling by inhibiting anti-inflammatory flavonoids, flavone glycosides, and caffeic acid derivatives that suppress pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Improved circulation results from the vasodilatory effect of its caffeic acid content, increasing blood flow to the affected areas. Enhanced detoxification and waste removal occur due to the diuretic properties of Equisetum arvense, stimulating the lymphatic system to efficiently eliminate toxins. Activating immune response is facilitated by its antimicrobial activity, which inhibits the growth of pathogenic bacteria and fungi. Regular consumption of Equisetum arvense also stimulates lymph node activity, enhances fluid balance, supports cell regeneration through its antioxidant properties, and accelerates the healing process by promoting tissue repair.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Equisetum arvense relieves joint inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory compounds, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, which inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce inflammation. It reduces muscle tension by relaxing muscle fibers and improving muscle blood flow, thanks to its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals. Equisetum arvense eases muscle spasms by modulating the neurotransmitter release of acetylcholine and increasing potassium ion levels, both of which contribute to muscle relaxation. The plant soothes tissue irritation with its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, thereby reducing tissue inflammation. Additionally, Equisetum arvense fights osteoarthritis by reducing cartilage degradation and promoting cartilage repair, alleviates back pain by reducing muscle tension and inflammation in the back, boosts bone density by stimulating osteoblast activity, improves joint mobility by reducing inflammation and promoting lubrication, treats fibromyalgia by reducing muscle pain and fatigue, and supports cartilage health by promoting cartilage repair and regeneration.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Equisetum arvense calms the nervous system by reducing the release of stress hormones, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated nerves. It also reduces anxiety levels by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, such as GABA, which helps regulate emotional responses. Furthermore, it relaxes muscle tension by inhibiting the contraction of muscle fibers, a property attributed to its spasmolytic and antispasmodic effects. Additionally, the antioxidants in Equisetum arvense prevent nerve damage by scavenging free radicals that can harm nerve cells, and improve sleep quality by regulating the body's circadian rhythms. Equisetum arvense also regulates nerve response, eases pain sensation, boosts nervous energy, protects brain cells, and enhances cognitive function due to its neuroprotective and nootropic properties.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Equisetum arvense regulates hormones by exerting estrogenic activity, which helps to balance hormone levels and prevent conditions such as endometriosis. It stimulates fertility by increasing sperm quality and motility, making it an effective treatment for infertility. Equisetum arvense enhances ovulation by increasing the production of follicle-stimulating hormone, which promotes the release of eggs from the ovaries and prevents miscarriage. Additionally, it relieves endometriosis symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can lead to scarring and adhesions in the reproductive tract. Equisetum arvense also supports menstruation, treats polycystic ovary syndrome, improves lactation, and alleviates amenorrhea by stimulating the production of prolactin and other hormones necessary for milk production and menstrual regulation.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Equisetum arvense alleviate respiratory issues by reducing inflammation, which is attributed to its anti-inflammatory properties that help to decrease swelling and ease breathing difficulty. The antimicrobial properties of Equisetum arvense prevent congestion by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms that can cause respiratory infections. Clearing mucus buildup is also a benefit of Equisetum arvense, achieved through its expectorant properties that help to loosen and remove excess mucus, ultimately improving oxygenation. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of Equisetum arvense soothe irritated tissues by neutralizing free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage. By strengthening lung function, eliminating coughs, and protecting lungs from damage, Equisetum arvense ensures that the respiratory system functions optimally.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Equisetum arvense prevents kidney damage by inhibiting the formation of renal calculi and reducing the risk of nephrotoxicity. It reduces bladder infections due to its antimicrobial properties that counteract the growth of bacteria such as Escherichia coli. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties treat urinary tract issues by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary system. Equisetum arvense also cures bladder incontinence and relieves prostate problems by toning and relaxing the muscles involved in urination. Additionally, it supports kidney function, tones urinary muscles, heals kidney stones, relaxes bladder muscles, and cleanses the urinary system by promoting the excretion of waste products and toxins.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Equisetum arvense are due to its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins in the plant. Equisetum arvense also displays antimicrobial properties, which are crucial in preventing the growth of various microorganisms, and these properties can be linked to the volatile oils present in the plant. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Equisetum arvense are responsible for neutralizing free radicals, thus protecting the body from oxidative stress, and this is mainly due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids. Furthermore, Equisetum arvense has been found to possess analgesic and anti-rheumatic properties, which are essential in treating pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism, and these properties can be attributed to the saponins and flavonoids in the plant. The plant also exhibits pain relief and diuretic properties, which are useful in treating various health conditions, including urinary retention, and these properties are mainly due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

properties_list.description: Equisetum arvense has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with various inflammatory conditions.
The plant has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which enable it to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, potentially helping to prevent infections.

Equisetum arvense has been identified as a source of antioxidants, which can neutralize free radicals and help protect cells from oxidative damage, potentially contributing to its overall health benefits.
The plant has been reported to have analgesic properties, which may help alleviate pain by interacting with pain receptors in the body or influencing the body's pain response mechanisms.

Equisetum arvense has been traditionally used to treat rheumatic conditions, and its anti-rheumatic properties may be attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with these conditions.

The plant has been found to provide relief from pain, which may be attributed to its anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and other bioactive compounds that interact with pain receptors and pathways in the body.
Equisetum arvense has been reported to have diuretic properties, which may help increase urine production and alleviate conditions characterized by fluid retention, such as edema.

constituents.description: Saponins are a class of glycosides characterized by their foaming and surface-active properties, typically containing a triterpenoid or steroid aglycone linked to one or more sugar molecules.
Phenolic acids from Equisetum arvense are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
Flavonoids in Equisetum arvense have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Coumarins are fragrant, plant-derived compounds that have anticoagulant properties and can inhibit blood clotting.
Sterols are a type of lipid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Equisetum arvense alkaloids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Triterpenoids are anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds found in Equisetum arvense, which may exhibit antiseptic and antiphlogistic properties.
Equisetum arvense contains minerals such as calcium, iron, magnesium, potassium, and silica, providing a range of ionic properties
Tannins are astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant compounds found in Equisetum arvense.
Equisetum arvense contains amino acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Equisetum arvense are saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, flavonoids including taxifolin and isorhapontigenin, which have been reported to display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, coumarins like scopoletin and is scopoletin, which have been found to possess antispasmodic and antioxidant activities, and sterols like beta-sitosterol, which has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, all contributing to its medicinal properties.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Equisetum arvense for medicinal purposes are leaves, roots, and rhizomes. The leaves contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and isorhapontigenin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The roots and rhizomes are rich in saponins, including equisetic and equisetonin, which have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, and also contain phenolic compounds like ferulic acid, which display antioxidant activity. The roots and rhizomes also contain volatile oils, including terpenes and sesquiterpenes, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive compounds in these parts contribute to the plant's traditional uses, including treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, coumarins, and sesquiterpenes, particularly isofraxidin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, providing relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing.

The roots of this plant are a rich source of alkaloids, especially equisetic acid, which possesses anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and alleviating pain and spasms.

The rhizomes of this plant contain furofuran lignans, including isofraxidin and desoxypsilocin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, providing relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat inflammation and pain, particularly in joints, and as a diuretic for urinary disorders such as kidney stones and incontinence.
preparations.overview: Equisetum arvense tincture is used to treat arthritis, rheumatism, inflammation, gout, muscle pain, sprains, muscle spasms, bruises, and tendinitis. It's also used to alleviate menstrual cramps. This preparation is common and widely used due to its effectiveness in reducing pain and inflammation. A common effect of Equisetum arvense tincture is the reduction of swelling and pain in joints and muscles. The preparation is made from the roots, rhizomes, and stems of the Equisetum arvense plant, which are combined with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create the tincture. Making Equisetum arvense tincture is moderately difficult as it requires specific skills and equipment to extract the active compounds from the plant material.
recipe: Gather 1 part of dried Equisetum arvense roots, 1 part dried rhizomes, and 2 parts dried stems.
Combine the dried Equisetum arvense parts in a clean glass jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, allowing the mixture to infuse and blend.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 80-proof vodka or a similar solvent to the liquid to create a 1:5 ratio, filling the container completely and sealing it to allow the tincture to mature for 4-6 weeks.
Once the tincture has matured, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle, with a suggested adult dosage of 20-40 drops, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat rheumatism, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as to lower blood pressure and improve kidney function.
preparations.overview: Equisetum arvense decoction is used to treat arthritis, rheumatism, gout, inflammation, sprains, muscle spasms, pain and urinary tract infections. It's a common preparation in herbal medicine, often used to address various health issues. The decoction typically has a diuretic effect, which is beneficial for conditions like kidney stones and urinary tract infections. This preparation is made from the roots, rhizomes, and stems of the Equisetum arvense plant, requiring a moderate level of difficulty to prepare. The process involves boiling the plant parts in water, creating a decoction that can be consumed to alleviate symptoms.
recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of Equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes, as well as 10-20 grams of dried stems.
Wash the roots, rhizomes, and stems thoroughly with cold water to remove any impurities.
Combine the cleaned roots, rhizomes, and stems in a pot and add 2 liters of cold water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, and take 50-100 milliliters up to 3 times a day as needed.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat rheumatism, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as to reduce swelling and pain in the joints, muscles, and skin.
preparations.overview: Equisetum arvense salve is a topical preparation commonly used to treat a variety of conditions including bruises, sprains, wounds, skin irritations, arthritis, insect bites, joint pain, burns, inflammation and muscle strains. It's a widely used remedy in herbal medicine. The common effect of using this salve is the reduction of inflammation and pain. This preparation is made from the roots, rhizomes and stems of the Equisetum arvense plant. The process of extracting the medicinal compounds from these plant parts is complex and time-consuming, making it a challenging task to produce a high-quality salve.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried Equisetum arvense roots and rhizomes, and 2 cups of dried stems, and store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried roots, rhizomes, and stems in a large pot, and pour in 2 cups of carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has been infused with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 drops of lavender essential oil to the infused oil, and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved, then pour the salve into tin containers and let it cool.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat inflammation, pain, and rheumatic conditions, and to lower blood pressure and improve kidney function.
preparations.overview: Equisetum arvense tea is a common herbal remedy used to treat a range of conditions including rheumatism, inflammation, arthritis, gout, pain, muscle spasms, fever, headaches, sprains and bruises. It's widely used due to its effectiveness and availability. One of its well-documented effects is the reduction of inflammation and pain. The tea is typically made from the rhizomes, roots, and stems of the Equisetum arvense plant, which are easily harvested and processed. The preparation process is straightforward and can be made at home by boiling the plant material in water.
recipe: Gather 1-2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense rhizomes and roots, and 2-3 stems for making the herbal tea.
Clean and chop the Equisetum arvense rhizomes, roots, and stems into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped rhizomes, roots, and stems in a saucepan with 2 cups of boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or a cup, discarding the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Equisetum arvense herbal tea 2-3 times a day, with a recommended dosage of 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb per 8 oz of water.

preparations.description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat joint pain, reduce inflammation, and relieve conditions such as rheumatism and gout.
preparations.overview: Equisetum arvense ointment is a preparation used to treat a variety of conditions including bruises, sprains, arthritis, skin irritation, muscle pain, tendinitis, muscle spasms, joint inflammation, wounds and joint pain. It's a common treatment option due to its widespread availability and frequent use. The common effect of this preparation is its anti-inflammatory action, which helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. This ointment is typically made from the rhizomes, roots and stems of the Equisetum arvense plant, which contain compounds with medicinal properties. However, preparing Equisetum arvense ointment can be challenging, as it requires careful harvesting and processing of the plant material to ensure its potency and effectiveness.
recipe: Combine 1 part of dried Equisetum arvense rhizomes, 1 part dried roots, and 2 parts dried stems to make a total of 4 parts of the herb mixture.
Steep the herb mixture in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of 1:2 (4 parts herb mixture to 8 parts carrier oil), for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids, and reserve the infused oil.
Add 1-2% of a preservative, such as vitamin E oil or grapefruit seed extract, to the infused oil to prevent spoilage, and mix well.

Store the Equisetum arvense herbal ointment, made by mixing the infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax, in a ratio of 10:1 (10 parts infused oil to 1 part beeswax), in a cool, dark place, and use a maximum of 3% of the infused oil per application.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Equisetum arvense for medicinal purposes are tincture and decoction, used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain relief, due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. A decoction is often used to treat urinary tract issues and kidney problems, by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the urinary system. A salve is applied topically to relieve skin irritations and wounds, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Tea is prepared from the plant and consumed to treat digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, by soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Additionally, an ointment is used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.

side_effects.side_effect_description: High doses of Equisetum arvense extracts have been reported to induce seizures in humans.
Ingestion of Equisetum arvense causes oxalate nephropathy, leading to kidney damage.
Consumption of Equisetum arvense causes goiter and hypothyroidism in animals and humans.
Equisetum arvense may increase the anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
Ingestion of Equisetum arvense has been associated with goiter and decreased thyroid function in animal studies.
Equisetum arvense increases renal workload and reduces nephron function in individuals with pre-existing kidney disease.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Equisetum arvense are experience seizures, a rare but potentially life-threatening condition that can occur when the plant's toxic compounds are ingested in large quantities. Developing kidney damage is another potential risk, particularly for individuals with pre-existing kidney issues or those taking medications that may exacerbate this condition. Exhibiting thyroid problems, such as an underactive thyroid, is also possible due to the plant's potential to interfere with thyroid hormone production. Interacting negatively with medications is a concern, as Equisetum arvense may enhance or inhibit the effects of certain prescription medications, leading to adverse reactions. Showing signs of hypothyroidism, including fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance, can occur when the plant's toxic compounds disrupt thyroid function.

precautions.precaution_description: Equisetum arvense can cause allergic reactions, interact with medications, or exacerbate underlying conditions in some individuals.
Equisetum arvense dose should be determined by a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential for toxicity and interactions with medications.
Individuals with known allergies to Equisetum arvense may experience allergic reactions upon exposure.
Equisetum arvense lowers blood sugar levels.
Equisetum arvense can lower blood pressure in people with hypertension due to its flavonoid and lignan content, which are vasodilators and have antihypertensive effects.
Equisetum arvense may exacerbate kidney problems due to its saponin and flavonoid content, particularly in individuals with pre-existing kidney issues.
People taking medications should consult a doctor before using Equisetum arvense due to potential interactions.
Equisetum arvense may cause bleeding due to its high silica content

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Equisetum arvense medicinally are to consult a doctor to ensure safe use, determine the dose to avoid overdose, identify potential allergies to prevent anaphylaxis, monitor blood sugar levels in diabetic patients, monitor blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, monitor kidney function in those with pre-existing kidney issues, obtain medical advice for pregnant and breastfeeding women, and watch for bleeding in patients taking anticoagulant medications.
identification: Equisetum arvense is a perennial vascular plant that belongs to the class Equisetopsida, and is characterized by its hollow, jointed stems, typically 1-2 meters tall, with whorled, strap-shaped leaves that are 1-5 centimeters long and have no teeth. It has a distinctive, nodular appearance at the nodes, where the leaves meet the stem. Equisetum arvense is found growing in a variety of habitats, including wet meadows, along streams, and in shallow water, throughout the Northern Hemisphere, with a wide distribution across North America, Europe, and Asia. It is particularly common in areas with alkaline soils and is often found in association with other plants such as sedges and grasses. In terms of its taxonomy, Equisetum arvense is a member of the genus Equisetum, which is a group of vascular plants that are thought to have originated during the Devonian period, over 380 million years ago.
cultivation: Equisetum arvense is a perennial plant that grows in dense colonies, often forming large stands in moist to wet environments, and typically reaches heights of 1-3 meters. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and requires well-drained to saturated soils, tolerating a wide pH range but preferring slightly acidic to neutral conditions. Growth tips for Equisetum arvense include maintaining consistent moisture levels, controlling competing vegetation, and harvesting the plant before it flowers to encourage regrowth. It can be grown in containers or directly in the ground, and can be propagated through division or seed, although seed germination is often low. Regular watering and fertilization are necessary for optimal growth, but excessive application can lead to reduced growth and increased susceptibility to disease.
history: Equisetum arvense is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Europe and Asia. The roots and rhizomes of the plant contain various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Historically, it has been used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and kidney problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and as a diuretic. In traditional folk medicine, it was often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects. The plant's medicinal uses were also documented in ancient texts, including the Greek physician Dioscorides' work "De Materia Medica", which dates back to the 1st century AD.
